task: Verify mobile menu toggle ARIA state changes in Navbar component
summary: The hamburger menu toggle button is missing critical ARIA state attributes. The `aria-expanded` attribute is absent in all states, and the `aria-label` remains static regardless of menu state.

checks.description: aria-expanded should be 'false' when mobile menu is closed
aria-expanded should be 'true' when mobile menu is open
aria-controls should reference the mobile menu element's id
checks.expected: aria-controls pointing to mobile menu id
checks.actual: aria-controls is missing (null); mobile menu also has no id attribute

checks.description: aria-label should change to reflect current action (e.g., '메뉴 닫기' when open)
checks.expected: aria-label changes between '메뉴 열기' (closed) and '메뉴 닫기' (open)
checks.actual: aria-label is always '메뉴 열기' regardless of menu state

checks.description: Mobile menu should appear/disappear when toggle is clicked
checks.expected: Menu appears on first click, disappears on second click
checks.actual: Menu correctly appears (data-testid='mobile-menu' rendered) on click and disappears on second click

checks.description: SVG icon should change between hamburger (3 lines) and X (close) icon
checks.expected: Icon changes to reflect open/closed state visually
checks.actual: Icon changes correctly (hamburger lines vs X path in SVG)

issues_found.issue: Missing aria-expanded attribute on hamburger toggle button
issues_found.impact: Screen reader users cannot determine whether the mobile menu is open or closed. This is a direct violation of WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2.
issues_found.recommendation: Add `aria-expanded={menuOpen}` to the hamburger button element.

issues_found.issue: Missing aria-controls attribute linking button to menu
issues_found.impact: Assistive technologies cannot programmatically associate the toggle button with the menu it controls.
issues_found.recommendation: Add an `id` to the mobile menu div (e.g., id="mobile-menu") and add `aria-controls="mobile-menu"` to the button.

issues_found.issue: Static aria-label does not reflect current menu state
issues_found.impact: The label always says '메뉴 열기' (Open menu) even when the menu is already open, which is misleading for screen reader users.

issues_found.issue: Mobile menu lacks role and aria-label
issues_found.impact: The mobile menu panel has no semantic role or label for assistive technologies.
issues_found.recommendation: Add `role="menu"` or use a nav landmark with `aria-label="모바일 메뉴"` to the mobile menu container.

source_code_note: Line 58 contains an explicit comment: '/* BAD: missing aria-expanded to indicate menu state */' confirming this is a known intentional defect in the test fixture.
